title: A Third Quarter Update to "The Purchasing Power of American Households"
summary: The Treasury Department released an analysis showing that in the year ending Q3 2024, median weekly real earnings rose 0.8% (3.3% annual rate), enabling the median American worker to afford the same goods and services as in 2019 plus an additional $1,600 to spend or save annually. Real weekly earnings have risen 2.8% since 2019 and 1.2% from a year prior, indicating sustained improvement in purchasing power.
key_points: Median weekly real earnings rose 0.8% in Q3 2024 (3.3% at annual rate), according to BLS data
Real weekly earnings increased 2.8% since 2019 for the typical middle-class worker
The median worker could afford 2019 consumption levels plus an additional $1,600/year
Data uses seasonally adjusted figures and four-quarter moving averages
Source incorporates CPI data through Q3 2024
why_it_matters: This Treasury analysis provides retrospective context on real wage growth and purchasing power trends. While not a market-moving catalyst, it informs understanding of labor market conditions and inflation's relative impact on household finances during the post-pandemic period.
macro_context: The data indicates that wage growth has broadly kept pace with inflation since 2019, with the median worker gaining purchasing power. This supports a narrative of resilient real income growth, though the analysis is backward-looking and based on data through Q3 2024. The $1,600 annual improvement figure represents cumulative gains since 2019.
important_numbers: 0.8% (Q3 2024 quarterly increase in median real earnings)
3.3% (annualized rate of real earnings growth in Q3 2024)
notes: Source is a Treasury blog post analyzing BLS earnings and CPI data. While it discusses macroeconomic conditions (real wages, purchasing power), it is not a macro catalyst in the trading sense—no policy decisions, rate announcements, or forward guidance are provided. The analysis is retrospective, covering data through Q3 2024, and does not constitute actionable market information.
